# Techniques for Creative Living

**Technique 1: Go Inside. Ask yourself: do I have to do this?**

**Technique 2: Be Patient. Do not rush your development.**

**Technique 3: Embrace Solitude. Spend time alone without distraction.**

**Case: The Daily Reflection. Rilke recommends regular time for stillness.**

**Case: The Reading. Read deeply, not widely. Let books change you.**

**Case: The Writing. Write only when you must. Do not force it.**
---

**Case: The Early Morning. Rilke recommends writing in the early morning, before the world intrudes.**
**Case: The Walk. A daily walk without destination allows the mind to wander.**
**Case: The Journal. Keep a journal of your inner life.**
**Case: The Letter. Write letters to people you care about. Express what you feel.**
**Case: The Poem a Day. Rilke did not recommend writing every day. He recommended living every day.**
**Case: The Travel. Travel broadens the artist's perspective.**
**Case: The Study. Study the works of great artists. Let them teach you.**
**Case: The Attention. Pay close attention to your own feelings and reactions.**
**Case: The Expression. Find your own voice. Do not imitate others.**
**Case: The Patience. Rilke says: let life happen to you. Trust that it will shape you.**
**Case: The Imagination. Feed your imagination with nature, art, and solitude.**
